description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with a Docker‑in‑Docker image (via .docker:build-docker-in-docker) and macOS runner images (via .installation:mac-os-job) that runs all jobs in a single "novel" stage. Add a novel:linux-amd64 job that executes ./support/docker ci-build-novel-image "${BUILD_OS_VERSION}" for each matrix value (arch and ubuntu) and follows the .rules:novel trigger. Add a novel:macos job that sets GDK_REPO_URL, echoes the install URL, curls the install script and pipes it to bash with ts logging, runs in the novel stage and uses variables GIT_STRATEGY=none, RUBY_OPENSSL_VERIFY_MODE=none, SSL_CERT_FILE=/etc/ssl/cert.pem. Add a postgres-upgrade:macos job that saves gdk-install.log, gitlab_log/ and gdk_log/ as artifacts, runs a series of shell commands to compare current and target PostgreSQL versions, modifies .tool-versions, runs gdk configuration and update commands, and is gated by .rules:postgres-upgrade. Finally, add an update:macos job that also archives gitlab_log/ and gdk_log/, runs ./support/install with ts logging, activates mise, sources utils.sh and runs support/ci/verify-update, also triggered by .rules:installation-changes.
